prompt: Our ML training loop runs for 6 hours. The model converges around hour 4 but we don't have early stopping configured properly. Additionally, data loading is the bottleneck for the first 30% of each epoch. Design a two-pronged optimization: faster data loading and smarter early stopping.

We have a monorepo with 15 microservices. When any service changes, all 15 get rebuilt and redeployed. Design a change-detection system that only rebuilds affected services and their dependents, with a measurement plan to prove it's correct.

Our mobile app's cold start time is 8 seconds on mid-range Android devices. The app initializes 12 SDKs, loads 3 databases, and fetches 5 API endpoints on launch. Prioritize which initializations to defer and design a lazy-loading strategy.

Our test suite is fast individually but slow in CI because of resource contention: 6 parallel test runners all hitting the same test database. Design an isolation and parallelization strategy that maintains test integrity while maximizing throughput.

We run a batch job that processes 10M records nightly. It currently takes 5 hours. The job is I/O bound on database reads, CPU bound on business logic, and I/O bound again on writing results. Design a pipelining approach that overlaps these phases.

Our GraphQL API has an N+1 problem that creates 200+ database queries for some complex nested queries. We already use DataLoader for some resolvers. Audit the query pattern and design a comprehensive batching and caching strategy with a before/after measurement plan.

Our DNS resolution is adding 50-100ms to every external API call. We make about 500 external calls per minute. Design a DNS caching and connection pooling strategy with proper TTL handling and fallback behavior.

We have a document indexing pipeline that processes PDFs. The current bottleneck is OCR, which takes 30 seconds per page. The pipeline processes 10,000 pages daily. Design an optimization strategy that might include parallel processing, smart queuing, and caching of previously-seen pages.
